title: Historical Operations Visibility Runtime Consumption First Browser Consumer Execution
historical_note: This record preserves one earlier Dev Residency Operations Visibility slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform on 2026-05-07. It is historical provenance, not a live Operations Visibility surface for current repo operations.

objective: Land the first governed runtime-consumption slice on top of the live `09` Operations Visibility schema tranche by exposing a read-only `v_master_operations` backend seam and mounting the first browser consumer in `apps/operations-web`.
dependency_note: Packet 047 made `public.v_master_operations` live in Supabase, so the next truthful slice was to consume that view through the already-governed control-plane boundary before widening any browser runtime further.
execution_steps: Add a read-only `GET /api/v1/ops/master-operations` seam in `apps/control-plane-api` against `public.v_master_operations`.
Validate that seam with a focused pytest file rather than widening into unrelated API or browser work first.
Add the first `apps/operations-web` helper and mounted browser consumer for the new route.
Validate the browser consumer with the narrowest available TypeScript check in the current environment.
constraints: Do not reopen direct browser-side Supabase admission.
Do not widen into unrelated dashboard scope, mutations, pagination redesign, or package/lockfile changes.
Keep the slice bounded to the new `v_master_operations` consumer path and its direct authority surfaces.

execution_evidence.diagnostics_status: No editor errors on touched backend and frontend files

execution_rationale: Once Packet 047 made the `09` tranche live, the smallest truthful next step was a governed runtime consumer rather than another planning-only loop.
`apps/control-plane-api/services/ops/router.py` already owned the read-only operator route boundary, so the first consumer belonged there instead of in a direct browser database client.
The existing browser shell already states that live reads should enter through backend-routed seams, so the mounted `MasterOperationsExplorer` extends that contract without widening authority.
